title: Pityrogramma elongata
taxonomy: Pityrogramma elongata is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Pityrogramma elongata is a vascular plant that belongs to the kingdom Plantae. This plant is further classified into the phylum Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. As a member of the family Pteridaceae, Pityrogramma elongata is closely related to other species of ferns in the same family.
uses: Pityrogramma elongata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported uses including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial applications. In some regions, the plant is used to treat skin conditions and wounds, while in others it is used to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including fever and respiratory issues.
